messages.content: {"claims":[{"index":0,"source":"src-obs","text":"Emitting one trace event per agent step lets teams reconstruct any run end to end."},{"index":1,"source":"src-obs","text":"Per-step tracing is the biggest lever on mean-time-to-diagnose for agent incidents."},{"index":2,"source":"src-eval","text":"A regression gate that blocks releases below an eval pass-rate threshold catches prompt drift."},{"index":3,"source":"src-eval","text":"Treating the golden set as a unit test for the agent surfaces defects before they ship."},{"index":4,"source":"src-cost","text":"Attributing token usage per agent role makes runaway orchestrator loops visible in the bill."},{"index":5,"source":"src-cost","text":"A per-role token budget is the cheapest cost guardrail and the first to pay off."},{"index":6,"source":"src-trust","text":"A groundedness check rejects any synthesized claim that lacks a source reference."},{"index":7,"source":"src-trust","text":"Requiring a citation per claim stops the agent from asserting facts it cannot cite."}]}

request.system: You are the research orchestrator. Group the worker-extracted claims into themes. Reply with ONLY a JSON object {"themes": [{"name": str, "claim_indexes": [int]}]}. Use a claim's index (its position in the input list). Cover every claim exactly once.
